name: Idas
description: Idas was a hero in Greek mythology, known for his strength, courage, and ultimately, his tragic fate. He was the son of Aphareus and Arene, and brother to Lynceus. Idas was a prince of Messenia, famed for his participation in the Calydonian Boar Hunt alongside many other Greek heroes. He is best known for his feud with the Dioscuri, Castor and Pollux, stemming from a dispute over the Leucippides, Phoebe and Hilaeira, daughters of Leucippus, who were promised to Idas and Lynceus, but desired by the Dioscuri. Idas and Lynceus abducted the Leucippides, leading to a fierce battle. In the ensuing conflict, Idas killed Castor, but was then struck down by Zeus's thunderbolt, avenging Castor's death and solidifying the Dioscuri's divine status. The story of Idas highlights themes of love, rivalry, vengeance, and the limitations of even the strongest mortal against the power of the gods. His tale serves as a cautionary reminder of hubris and the consequences of defying divine will. He was also known for his participation in the Argonaut expedition, further cementing his status as a significant figure in Greek heroic lore. While not always portrayed as a virtuous hero, Idas's strength and determination made him a formidable opponent, and his tragic end contributes to the complex tapestry of Greek mythology.
subtitle: Messenian Prince, Tragic Hero
achievements: Participated in the Calydonian Boar Hunt
Proved mortal limitations against divine power

keyMyths.title: The Abduction of the Leucippides
keyMyths.description: Idas and his brother Lynceus abducted Phoebe and Hilaeira, daughters of Leucippus, who were promised to them but desired by Castor and Pollux. This act ignited a bitter feud between the two pairs of brothers.

keyMyths.title: The Death of Castor and Idas
keyMyths.description: During the conflict over the Leucippides, Idas killed Castor, one of the Dioscuri. Zeus intervened, striking Idas down with a thunderbolt, avenging Castor's death.

keyMyths.title: Idas and the Argonauts
keyMyths.description: Idas was a member of the Argonauts, the band of heroes who sailed with Jason in search of the Golden Fleece. He contributed his strength and skill to the perilous journey.

sources.author: Apollodorus
